title: Maturation Score Workflow
text: Calculating a PCA-based Maturation Score to quantify differentiation progress. Basically projecting samples onto a vector defined by the D21 control timecourse (Day 6 -> Day 17).

title: Maturation Score Workflow
text:
A. 3D PCA
plotting the control trajectory


Code
# Var explained
var_exp <- summary(pca_res)$importance[2, 1:3] * 100
pc1_lab <- paste0("PC1: ", round(var_exp[1], 1), "%")
pc2_lab <- paste0("PC2: ", round(var_exp[2], 1), "%")
pc3_lab <- paste0("PC3: ", round(var_exp[3], 1), "%")

# Filter for control only
pca_coords_ctrl <- pca_coords %>% filter(!!sym(group_col) == control_group)

# Colors (no black for Day 6)
cols <- c("#1B9E77", "#D95F02", "#7570B3")
cols_mapped <- cols[as.numeric(pca_coords_ctrl[[time_col]])]

# define plotting function to avoid duplication
plot_pca_3d <- function() {
  # Scatterplot3d with manual margin adjustments
  s3d <- scatterplot3d(x = pca_coords_ctrl$PC1, 
                       y = pca_coords_ctrl$PC2, 
                       z = pca_coords_ctrl$PC3,
                       color = cols_mapped,
                       pch = 19, 
                       type = "h", 
                       main = paste("3D PCA (", control_group, ")"),
                       xlab = pc1_lab,
                       ylab = pc2_lab,
                       zlab = pc3_lab,
                       angle = 45,
                       scale.y = 0.7,
                       y.margin.add = 0.2,
                       mar = c(5, 5, 4, 7))
  
  # Arrow
  s3d_coords <- s3d$xyz.convert(x = c(origin_vec[1], endpoint_vec[1]), 
                                y = c(origin_vec[2], endpoint_vec[2]), 
                                z = c(origin_vec[3], endpoint_vec[3]))
  
  arrows(x0 = s3d_coords$x[1], y0 = s3d_coords$y[1], 
         x1 = s3d_coords$x[2], y1 = s3d_coords$y[2], 
         lwd = 3, col = "black", length = 0.1)
  
  text(s3d_coords$x[1], s3d_coords$y[1], labels = "Start", pos = 1, cex = 1.2)
  text(s3d_coords$x[2], s3d_coords$y[2], labels = "End", pos = 3, cex = 1.2)
  
  # Legend outside
  par(xpd = TRUE)
  legend("topright", inset = c(-0.15, 0), legend = levels(pca_coords_ctrl[[time_col]]),
         col = cols, pch = 19, title = "Timepoint", bty = "n", cex = 1.0)
}

# Save plot (Smaller dimensions)
png("plots/pca_trajectory_plot.png", width = 6, height = 5, units = "in", res = 300)
plot_pca_3d()
dev.off()


unigd 
    2 


Code
# show plot in rmd
plot_pca_3d()

title: Maturation Score Workflow
text:
B. Scores
Barplot and Lineplot.


Code
# summary stats
score_sum <- pca_coords %>%
  group_by(!!sym(group_col), !!sym(time_col)) %>%
  summarise(
    mean = mean(maturation_score),
    se = sd(maturation_score) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 1. Bbarplot
p_bar <- ggplot(score_sum, aes(x = !!sym(time_col), y = mean, fill = !!sym(group_col))) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se), 
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.2) +
  geom_point(data = pca_coords, 
             aes(x = !!sym(time_col), y = maturation_score, group = !!sym(group_col)),
             position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.2, dodge.width = 0.8), 
             color = "black", size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(y = "Norm Score", title = "Maturation Score", fill = "Genotype")

# 2. lineplot
p_line <- ggplot(score_sum, aes(x = !!sym(time_col), y = mean, group = !!sym(group_col), color = !!sym(group_col))) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se), width = 0.1, size = 0.5) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(y = "Norm Score", title = "Trajectory", color = "Genotype")

grid.arrange(p_bar, p_line, ncol = 2)

title: Differentiation Progression Analysis
text:
This workflow quantifies how differentiation timing differs between experimental conditions using bulk RNA-seq data. It identifies temporally variable genes via the likelihood ratio test, constructs a reference trajectory through PCA space using control samples, and projects all samples onto that trajectory to compute a scalar maturation score.

Dataset. Publicly available data from Martinez et al. (2024): D21 (euploid control) and T21 (trisomy 21) iPSC-derived neural progenitors sampled at Days 6, 10, and 17 of differentiation. 18 samples total (3 replicates per genotype per timepoint).

title: Differentiation Progression Analysis
text:
This workflow quantifies how differentiation timing differs between experimental conditions using bulk RNA-seq data. It identifies temporally variable genes via the likelihood ratio test, constructs a reference trajectory through PCA space using control samples, and projects all samples onto that trajectory to compute a scalar maturation score.

Dataset. Publicly available data from Martinez et al. (2024): D21 (euploid control) and T21 (trisomy 21) iPSC-derived neural progenitors sampled at Days 6, 10, and 17 of differentiation. 18 samples total (3 replicates per genotype per timepoint).

title: Differentiation Progression Analysis
text:
Mathematical Framework

Negative Binomial GLM
DESeq2 models read counts with a negative binomial distribution, where each gene \(i\) in sample \(j\) follows:
\[
K_{ij} \sim \text{NB}\!\left(\mu_{ij},\; \alpha_i\right)
\]
\[
\log_2(\mu_{ij}) = \sum_r \beta_r^{(i)}\, x_{jr}
\]
where \(\alpha_i\) is the gene-specific dispersion and \(x_{jr}\) are design matrix entries.


Likelihood Ratio Test
The LRT compares a full model (including timepoint) against a reduced model (intercept only):
\[
D = -2\left[
  \ell\!\left(\hat{\boldsymbol{\theta}}_{\text{reduced}}\right)
  - \ell\!\left(\hat{\boldsymbol{\theta}}_{\text{full}}\right)
\right]
\;\sim\; \chi^2_{df_{\text{full}} - df_{\text{reduced}}}
\]
Genes with significant \(D\) statistics (after BH correction) change expression across timepoints.


PCA
Given a centered expression matrix \(\mathbf{X}_c\) (genes \(\times\) samples), PCA computes the singular value decomposition:
\[
\mathbf{X}_c = \mathbf{U}\boldsymbol{\Sigma}\mathbf{V}^\top
\]
Each sample’s coordinates in PC space:
\[
\mathbf{z}_i
= \mathbf{V}^\top
  \!\left(\mathbf{x}_i - \bar{\mathbf{x}}\right)
\]


Centroid Vector Projection
The reference vector connects control centroids at the earliest and latest timepoints:
\[
\vec{v}
= \bar{\mathbf{z}}_{t_{\text{late}}}
  - \bar{\mathbf{z}}_{t_{\text{early}}}
\qquad\text{where}\quad
\bar{\mathbf{z}}_t
= \frac{1}{n_t}\sum_{i \in t}\mathbf{z}_i
\]


Maturation Score
Each sample is projected onto the reference vector and normalized so the early centroid maps to 0 and the late centroid maps to 1:
\[
s_i
= \frac{
    \left(\mathbf{z}_i
      - \bar{\mathbf{z}}_{t_{\text{early}}}\right)
    \cdot \vec{v}
  }{
    \lVert\vec{v}\rVert^2
  }
\]
Scores below 0 indicate less-differentiated-than-early control; scores above 1 indicate more-differentiated-than-late control.

title: Differentiation Progression Analysis
text:
Analysis

Setup


Code
library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(plotly)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)

meta <- readRDS("dat/metadata/WC24_metadata_clean.rds")
raw_counts <- readRDS(
  "dat/counts/raw/WC24_filt_raw_counts.rds"
)
vst_counts <- readRDS(
  "dat/counts/vst/WC24_vst_counts.rds"
)

meta$timepoint <- factor(
  meta$timepoint, levels = c(6, 10, 17)
)

cat(
  "Samples:", nrow(meta), "\n",
  "Genes:", nrow(raw_counts), "\n",
  "Genotypes:", paste(levels(factor(meta$genotype)),
                      collapse = ", "), "\n",
  "Timepoints:", paste(levels(meta$timepoint),
                       collapse = ", ")
)


Samples: 18 
 Genes: 13826 
 Genotypes: D21, T21 
 Timepoints: 6, 10, 17




Feature Selection: Likelihood Ratio Test
The LRT is run on D21 control samples only. The full model includes timepoint; the reduced model is intercept-only. Genes where timepoint significantly explains variance are temporally variable.


Code
# Subset to D21 controls
ctrl_meta <- meta[meta$genotype == "D21", ]
ctrl_counts <- raw_counts[, ctrl_meta$sample_id]

# DESeq2 LRT
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = ctrl_counts,
  colData   = ctrl_meta,
  design    = ~ timepoint
)
dds <- DESeq(dds, test = "LRT", reduced = ~ 1)
res <- results(dds)
res <- res[order(res$padj), ]

# Significant genes
sig_genes <- rownames(res)[
  !is.na(res$padj) & res$padj < 0.05
]

cat(
  "Significant genes (padj < 0.05):",
  length(sig_genes), "\n",
  "Top 10:", paste(head(sig_genes, 10), collapse = ", ")
)


Significant genes (padj < 0.05): 8299 
 Top 10: SYT4, GRIK3, NKX2-1, VWF, ZFP42, HHIP, JAKMIP2-AS1, ST18, RARG, DMKN


Top 1000 genes (by adjusted p-value) are used for PCA and trajectory definition; all significant genes are shown in the heatmap below.


Code
n_top <- min(1000, length(sig_genes))
top_genes <- head(rownames(res), n_top)
cat("Genes used for PCA:", n_top)


Genes used for PCA: 1000




Temporal Gene Heatmap
Z-scored VST expression of all temporally variable genes across D21 samples, ordered by timepoint. Values are winsorized at \(\pm 2\).


Code
# Z-score and winsorize
hm_mat <- as.matrix(
  vst_counts[sig_genes, ctrl_meta$sample_id]
)
hm_z <- t(scale(t(hm_mat)))
hm_z[hm_z >  2] <-  2
hm_z[hm_z < -2] <- -2

# Column annotation
col_anno <- HeatmapAnnotation(
  Day = factor(
    ctrl_meta[colnames(hm_z), "timepoint"],
    levels = c(6, 10, 17)
  ),
  col = list(Day = c(
    "6"  = "#93C5FD",
    "10" = "#3B82F6",
    "17" = "#1E3A8A"
  )),
  annotation_name_side = "left",
  show_legend = TRUE
)

col_fun <- colorRamp2(
  c(-2, 0, 2), c("#2166AC", "#F7F7F7", "#B2182B")
)

ht <- Heatmap(
  hm_z,
  name              = "Z-score",
  col               = col_fun,
  top_annotation    = col_anno,
  cluster_columns   = FALSE,
  cluster_rows      = TRUE,
  clustering_method_rows = "ward.D2",
  show_row_names    = FALSE,
  show_column_names = TRUE,
  column_split      = factor(
    ctrl_meta[colnames(hm_z), "timepoint"],
    levels = c(6, 10, 17)
  ),
  column_title      = c("Day 6", "Day 10", "Day 17"),
  column_gap        = unit(3, "mm"),
  row_title         = paste0(
    length(sig_genes), " genes"
  ),
  border            = TRUE,
  use_raster        = TRUE
)

draw(ht)








Figure 1: Heatmap of temporally variable genes in D21 control samples







Example Temporal Patterns
Three representative genes illustrating distinct temporal behaviors identified by the LRT.


Code
# Mean VST per timepoint for each sig gene
ctrl_vst <- vst_counts[sig_genes, ctrl_meta$sample_id]
tp_num <- as.numeric(
  as.character(ctrl_meta$timepoint)
)

mean_d6  <- rowMeans(ctrl_vst[, tp_num == 6])
mean_d10 <- rowMeans(ctrl_vst[, tp_num == 10])
mean_d17 <- rowMeans(ctrl_vst[, tp_num == 17])

# Classify temporal patterns
peak_early <- names(which(
  mean_d6 > mean_d10 & mean_d10 > mean_d17
))
mono_inc <- names(which(
  mean_d6 < mean_d10 & mean_d10 < mean_d17
))
peak_mid <- names(which(
  mean_d10 > mean_d6 & mean_d10 > mean_d17
))

# Dynamic range for picking representative genes
dyn_range <- apply(
  cbind(mean_d6, mean_d10, mean_d17), 1,
  function(x) max(x) - min(x)
)

gene_early <- peak_early[
  which.max(dyn_range[peak_early])
]
gene_inc <- mono_inc[
  which.max(dyn_range[mono_inc])
]
gene_mid <- peak_mid[
  which.max(dyn_range[peak_mid])
]

example_genes <- c(gene_early, gene_mid, gene_inc)
pattern_names <- c(
  "Declining from Day 6",
  "Peak at Day 10",
  "Monotonic Increase"
)

# Build plot data
plot_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(
  seq_along(example_genes), function(i) {
    g <- example_genes[i]
    data.frame(
      gene      = g,
      pattern   = pattern_names[i],
      timepoint = tp_num,
      expr      = as.numeric(ctrl_vst[g, ]),
      sample    = ctrl_meta$sample_id
    )
  }
))

plot_df$label <- paste0(
  plot_df$pattern, "\n(", plot_df$gene, ")"
)
plot_df$label <- factor(
  plot_df$label,
  levels = unique(plot_df$label)
)

# Compute means
plot_means <- plot_df %>%
  group_by(label, timepoint) %>%
  summarize(
    m  = mean(expr),
    se = sd(expr) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot() +
  geom_point(
    data = plot_df,
    aes(x = timepoint, y = expr),
    color = "#94A3B8", size = 2, alpha = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    data = plot_means,
    aes(x = timepoint, ymin = m - se, ymax = m + se),
    width = 0.8, color = "#1E40AF", linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = plot_means,
    aes(x = timepoint, y = m),
    color = "#1E40AF", linewidth = 1
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = plot_means,
    aes(x = timepoint, y = m),
    color = "#1E40AF", size = 3
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ label, nrow = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(6, 10, 17),
    labels = c("Day 6", "Day 10", "Day 17")
  ) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "VST Expression") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(
      size = 10, face = "bold"
    ),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )








Figure 2: Example genes showing three temporal patterns: declining from Day 6, peaking at Day 10, and monotonically increasing







PCA: Samples in PC Space
PCA is computed on D21 controls using the top LRT genes. All 18 samples are then projected into this control-defined space.


Code
# PCA on D21 controls
pca_input <- t(as.matrix(
  vst_counts[top_genes, ctrl_meta$sample_id]
))
pca_res <- prcomp(pca_input, scale. = TRUE,
                  center = TRUE)

# Variance explained
var_pct <- round(
  summary(pca_res)$importance[2, 1:3] * 100, 1
)

# Project all samples
all_input <- t(as.matrix(
  vst_counts[top_genes, meta$sample_id]
))
all_proj <- predict(pca_res, all_input)

# Build data frame
pca_df <- data.frame(
  PC1       = all_proj[, 1],
  PC2       = all_proj[, 2],
  PC3       = all_proj[, 3],
  sample    = meta$sample_id,
  genotype  = meta$genotype,
  timepoint = paste0("Day ", meta$timepoint)
)

# Color + symbol maps
geno_colors <- c("D21" = "#3B82F6", "T21" = "#EF4444")
tp_symbols  <- c(
  "Day 6" = "circle", "Day 10" = "square",
  "Day 17" = "diamond"
)

plot_ly(
  pca_df,
  x      = ~PC1, y = ~PC2, z = ~PC3,
  color  = ~genotype,
  symbol = ~timepoint,
  colors = geno_colors,
  symbols = tp_symbols,
  type   = "scatter3d",
  mode   = "markers",
  marker = list(size = 6, opacity = 0.85),
  text   = ~paste(
    sample, "<br>",
    genotype, timepoint
  ),
  hoverinfo = "text"
) %>%
  layout(scene = list(
    xaxis = list(title = paste0(
      "PC1 (", var_pct[1], "%)"
    )),
    yaxis = list(title = paste0(
      "PC2 (", var_pct[2], "%)"
    )),
    zaxis = list(title = paste0(
      "PC3 (", var_pct[3], "%)"
    ))
  ))








Figure 3: Interactive 3D PCA of all samples projected into the D21 control-defined PC space






Reference Vector and Centroid Projection
The reference trajectory is defined by connecting the D21 Day 6 centroid to the D21 Day 17 centroid in PC space. Each centroid is the mean of the replicate coordinates:
\[
\bar{\mathbf{z}}_{\text{Day}\,6}
= \frac{1}{3}\sum_{i=1}^{3}\mathbf{z}_i^{(\text{D21, D6})}
\qquad
\vec{v}
= \bar{\mathbf{z}}_{\text{Day}\,17}
  - \bar{\mathbf{z}}_{\text{Day}\,6}
\]


Code
# Centroids
tp_num_all <- as.numeric(as.character(meta$timepoint))
d6_idx  <- meta$genotype == "D21" & tp_num_all == 6
d17_idx <- meta$genotype == "D21" & tp_num_all == 17

centroid_d6  <- colMeans(all_proj[d6_idx,  1:3])
centroid_d17 <- colMeans(all_proj[d17_idx, 1:3])
ref_vec <- centroid_d17 - centroid_d6

cat(
  "Day 6 centroid: ",
  paste(round(centroid_d6, 2), collapse = ", "), "\n",
  "Day 17 centroid:",
  paste(round(centroid_d17, 2), collapse = ", "), "\n",
  "Vector magnitude:",
  round(sqrt(sum(ref_vec^2)), 2)
)


Day 6 centroid:  -28.37, -11.32, -0.07 
 Day 17 centroid: 35.98, -5.47, -0.03 
 Vector magnitude: 64.62




Code
# Sample points
p <- plot_ly() %>%
  # D21 samples
  add_trace(
    data   = pca_df[pca_df$genotype == "D21", ],
    x = ~PC1, y = ~PC2, z = ~PC3,
    type   = "scatter3d", mode = "markers",
    marker = list(
      size = 5, color = "#3B82F6", opacity = 0.7
    ),
    name = "D21",
    text = ~paste(sample, timepoint),
    hoverinfo = "text"
  ) %>%
  # T21 samples
  add_trace(
    data   = pca_df[pca_df$genotype == "T21", ],
    x = ~PC1, y = ~PC2, z = ~PC3,
    type   = "scatter3d", mode = "markers",
    marker = list(
      size = 5, color = "#EF4444", opacity = 0.7
    ),
    name = "T21",
    text = ~paste(sample, timepoint),
    hoverinfo = "text"
  ) %>%
  # Centroids
  add_trace(
    x = c(centroid_d6[1], centroid_d17[1]),
    y = c(centroid_d6[2], centroid_d17[2]),
    z = c(centroid_d6[3], centroid_d17[3]),
    type   = "scatter3d", mode = "markers",
    marker = list(
      size = 10, color = "#059669",
      symbol = "diamond", opacity = 1
    ),
    name = "Centroids",
    text = c("Day 6 Centroid", "Day 17 Centroid"),
    hoverinfo = "text"
  ) %>%
  # Reference vector line
  add_trace(
    x = c(centroid_d6[1], centroid_d17[1]),
    y = c(centroid_d6[2], centroid_d17[2]),
    z = c(centroid_d6[3], centroid_d17[3]),
    type = "scatter3d", mode = "lines",
    line = list(width = 8, color = "#059669"),
    name = "Reference Vector",
    showlegend = FALSE
  ) %>%
  # Arrow tip (cone)
  add_trace(
    type = "cone",
    x = centroid_d17[1],
    y = centroid_d17[2],
    z = centroid_d17[3],
    u = ref_vec[1] * 0.15,
    v = ref_vec[2] * 0.15,
    w = ref_vec[3] * 0.15,
    sizemode  = "absolute",
    sizeref   = 3,
    anchor    = "tail",
    showscale = FALSE,
    colorscale = list(
      c(0, "#059669"), c(1, "#059669")
    ),
    name = "",
    showlegend = FALSE
  ) %>%
  layout(scene = list(
    xaxis = list(title = paste0(
      "PC1 (", var_pct[1], "%)"
    )),
    yaxis = list(title = paste0(
      "PC2 (", var_pct[2], "%)"
    )),
    zaxis = list(title = paste0(
      "PC3 (", var_pct[3], "%)"
    ))
  ))

p








Figure 4: Reference vector (green) from Day 6 to Day 17 centroid. Experimental samples are projected onto this vector to compute maturation scores.






Maturation Scores
Each sample is projected onto \(\vec{v}\) and normalized:
\[
s_i
= \frac{
    \left(\mathbf{z}_i
      - \bar{\mathbf{z}}_{\text{Day}\,6}\right)
    \cdot \vec{v}
  }{
    \lVert\vec{v}\rVert^2
  }
\]
where \(s \approx 0\) at Day 6 and \(s \approx 1\) at Day 17 for D21 controls by construction.


Code
# Compute scores
scores <- apply(all_proj[, 1:3], 1, function(z) {
  sum((z - centroid_d6) * ref_vec) / sum(ref_vec^2)
})

score_df <- data.frame(
  sample     = meta$sample_id,
  genotype   = meta$genotype,
  timepoint  = paste0("Day ", meta$timepoint),
  score      = scores
)
score_df$timepoint <- factor(
  score_df$timepoint,
  levels = c("Day 6", "Day 10", "Day 17")
)

# Summary table
score_summary <- score_df %>%
  group_by(genotype, timepoint) %>%
  summarize(
    mean_score = round(mean(score), 3),
    sd         = round(sd(score), 3),
    .groups    = "drop"
  )

knitr::kable(
  score_summary,
  col.names = c(
    "Genotype", "Timepoint", "Mean Score", "SD"
  ),
  caption = "Maturation scores by condition"
)



Maturation scores by condition


Genotype
Timepoint
Mean Score
SD




D21
Day 6
0.000
0.015


D21
Day 10
0.359
0.039


D21

1.000

Day 6
0.128
0.015


T21

0.489
0.031


T21

0.950
0.031







Code
ggplot(
  score_df,
  aes(x = timepoint, y = score, fill = genotype)
) +
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.5, outlier.shape = NA,
    alpha = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_point(
    position = position_jitterdodge(
      jitter.width = 0.05, dodge.width = 0.5
    ),
    size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8,
    aes(color = genotype)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "D21" = "#3B82F6", "T21" = "#EF4444"
  )) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "D21" = "#2563EB", "T21" = "#DC2626"
  )) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = c(0, 1),
    linetype = "dashed", color = "#94A3B8"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text", x = 0.55, y = 0.03,
    label = "Day 6 ref", color = "#94A3B8",
    size = 3, hjust = 0
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text", x = 0.55, y = 1.03,
    label = "Day 17 ref", color = "#94A3B8",
    size = 3, hjust = 0
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL, y = "Maturation Score",
    fill = "Genotype", color = "Genotype"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "top"
  )








Figure 5: Maturation scores by genotype and timepoint. Scores near 0 = Day 6 control level; scores near 1 = Day 17 control level.







Code
# Mean trajectory
traj_df <- score_df %>%
  group_by(genotype, timepoint) %>%
  summarize(
    mean_score = mean(score),
    se = sd(score) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot(traj_df, aes(
  x = timepoint, y = mean_score,
  color = genotype, group = genotype
)) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(
      ymin = mean_score - se,
      ymax = mean_score + se,
      fill = genotype
    ),
    alpha = 0.15, color = NA
  ) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_point(
    data = score_df,
    aes(x = timepoint, y = score),
    alpha = 0.4, size = 1.5
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "D21" = "#3B82F6", "T21" = "#EF4444"
  )) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "D21" = "#3B82F6", "T21" = "#EF4444"
  )) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = c(0, 1),
    linetype = "dashed", color = "#CBD5E1"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL, y = "Maturation Score",
    color = "Genotype", fill = "Genotype"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "top"
  )








Figure 6: Differentiation trajectories for D21 and T21







Genotype Comparison


Code
# Compute differences
d21_means <- score_summary %>%
  filter(genotype == "D21") %>%
  select(timepoint, d21 = mean_score)
t21_means <- score_summary %>%
  filter(genotype == "T21") %>%
  select(timepoint, t21 = mean_score)

diff_df <- inner_join(
  d21_means, t21_means, by = "timepoint"
) %>%
  mutate(
    difference = round(t21 - d21, 3),
    pct_diff   = paste0(
      round(difference * 100, 1), "%"
    )
  )

knitr::kable(
  diff_df,
  col.names = c(
    "Timepoint", "D21 Score", "T21 Score",
    "Difference", "% Difference"
  ),
  caption = paste(
    "Maturation score differences",
    "(T21 - D21)"
  )
)

Positive differences indicate T21 is further along the control differentiation trajectory; negative differences indicate T21 is behind. The results suggest T21 neural progenitors differentiate faster early but fail to reach the D21 endpoint by Day 17.

Analysis based on data from Martinez et al. (2024). Stem Cell Reports 17(6): 1380-1396.
